post.description: Authenticates with email + password. Sets an HTTP-Only refresh token cookie scoped to /api/v1/auth.

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

post.description: Reads the refresh_token HTTP-Only cookie and issues a new short-lived access token.

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

post.description: Revokes current session and clears the refresh cookie.
Validates Telegram WebApp initData HMAC signature and logs in the linked CRM user.

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

post.description: Completes onboarding for an invited user: validates invitation token + Telegram initData, creates user, sets session.

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

get.description: Returns all non-revoked, non-expired sessions for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Revokes ALL sessions for the current user and clears the refresh cookie.
Remote-wipe a specific session by UUID (sign out a device).

post.description: Directly creates a CRM user without an invitation (SUPERADMIN only).
Sends a 4-digit OTP to the provided email. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration. Rate-limited to 3 requests per 15 minutes.

200.description: OTP sent (or silently ignored if email not found)

message.example: If this email is registered, a reset code has been sent

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

post.description: Validates the 4-digit OTP and sets a new password. Max 3 failed attempts — the code is invalidated after 3 failures or on success.

message.example: Password has been reset. Please log in.

400.description: Bad request – invalid input or business rule violation

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

patch.description: Authenticated users can change their own password by providing their current password. All sessions are revoked on success.

400.description: Bad request – invalid input or business rule violation

429.description: Too many requests – rate limit exceeded

get.description: Returns all CRM system users. Requires ADMIN role or higher.
Returns all pending and accepted invitations.

patch.description: Deactivates the user and instantly revokes all their sessions (SUPERADMIN only).

204.description: User deactivated and all sessions revoked

patch.description: Resets a user's password and revokes all their sessions (SUPERADMIN only).

204.description: Password changed and all sessions revoked

post.description: Creates an invitation with a Telegram deep link for onboarding a new CRM user.

patch.description: Re-enables a deactivated user and clears their Redis block, restoring access (SUPERADMIN only).
Updates a user's role. Cannot change own role (SUPERADMIN only).

400.description: Bad request – invalid input or business rule violation

post.description: Directly creates a CRM user without an invitation (SUPERADMIN only).

get.description: Returns all CRM system users. Requires SUPERADMIN role.
Returns a specific user by UUID (SUPERADMIN only).

delete.description: Deactivates the user and instantly revokes all their sessions (SUPERADMIN only).

patch.description: Resets a user's password and revokes all their sessions (SUPERADMIN only).
Re-enables a deactivated user and clears their Redis block, restoring access (SUPERADMIN only).
Updates a user's role. Cannot change own role (SUPERADMIN only).

post.description: Public endpoint: a lead submits their email and/or HFM Broker ID via Telegram bot.

get.description: Paginated list of all leads with optional status filter. All roles can view leads (flat access model).
Returns a lead and its full interaction history.

patch.description: Enables/disables human handover mode for a lead. Instantly synced to Redis for bot awareness.
Owner manually verifies the registration/deposit proof. Sets verifiedAt timestamp.

get.description: Leads ordered by aiScore DESC. Tier: hot≥70, warm≥40, cold<40.
Streams a CSV file of all leads matching optional filters.

get.summary: Paginated interaction timeline for a lead

get.description: Subscribe with ?kbId=<id> to receive real-time processing updates.

200.description: SSE stream of { status, progress, kbId?, error? }

get.summary: SSE stream for KB file processing status

post.description: File is processed asynchronously. Subscribe to GET /knowledge-base/status?kbId=<id> for real-time progress.
post.summary: Upload file to KB (PDF, DOCX, image)

get.description: Returns all command menus ordered by display order.

post.description: Creates a new command menu with Tiptap rich content. Embedding generated asynchronously.

patch.description: Updates label, description, content, order, or isActive. Re-generates embedding if content changes.
Accepts array of { id, order } to update display positions.

get.description: Returns all uploaded receipts/screenshots for the given lead UUID.

503.description: The Health Check is not successful

get.description: Returns consolidated KPI cards, funnel metrics, and trend series data based on a given timeframe.

parameters.name: timeframe
parameters.description: The predefined timeframe for the analytics.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Required if timeframe is custom (ISO 8601 date string)

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Required if timeframe is custom (ISO 8601 date string)

get.description: Returns lead funnel counts. Use /analytics/summary instead.
Returns today's aggregated stats. Returns null if daily stats haven't been computed yet.
Returns new leads, registered leads, and deposits grouped by ISO week. Default: last 8 weeks.

parameters.name: weeks
parameters.description: Number of past weeks to include (default 8)

get.description: Hit rate, avg chunks, zero-hit count, token usage from last N auto-reply interactions.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of recent replies to analyse (default 500)

delete.summary: Cancel a scheduled follow-up by ID

get.summary: Get a single config entry by key

patch.summary: Upsert a config value (validated against allowlist)

delete.summary: Reset config key to env default (delete DB override)

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream. Connect with EventSource. Keepalive ping every 30s.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Bearer token (alternative to Authorization header for EventSource)

post.summary: Submit conversation feedback (rate a bot reply)

patch.summary: Toggle usedAsFewShot on a feedback entry (SUPERADMIN)

info.title: Titan Journal CRM API
info.description: **Titan Journal** — Enterprise Telegram IB CRM REST API

isActive.description: Whether this account is active and can log in

telegramId.description: Telegram user ID linked for TMA login. Null if not linked.

lastIpAddress.description: IP address from the last login

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT access token (15 min). Send as Authorization: Bearer <token>

ipAddress.description: Last known IP address for this session

expiresAt.description: When the refresh token expires (7 days from creation)

isRevoked.description: True if this session has been manually revoked

initData.description: Telegram WebApp initData string from window.Telegram.WebApp.initData

invitationToken.description: Invitation token starting with inv_ received via Telegram deep link

initData.description: Telegram WebApp initData string from window.Telegram.WebApp.initData

email.description: Email to set for this new account

deviceId.description: Unique device identifier for session tracking

role.description: RBAC role to assign to this user

confirmPassword.description: Repeat new password — must match newPassword

role.description: Role that will be assigned upon account setup

email.description: Pre-filled email (if provided during invite creation)

telegramDeepLink.description: Telegram deep link the invited user must open. Embeds the invitation token.

expiresAt.description: Invitation expires 7 days after creation. Invalid after this timestamp.

role.description: Role to assign to the invited user

email.description: Pre-fill email for the invited user (optional). They can set it during setup.

role.description: New role to assign to the user

telegramUserId.description: Telegram user ID (serialised as string due to BigInt)

hfmBrokerId.description: HFM broker account ID submitted by the lead

email.description: Email address submitted by the lead

phoneNumber.description: Phone number submitted by the lead

depositBalance.description: Lifetime deposit balance as decimal string (Prisma Decimal serialised)

registeredAt.description: When the lead submitted registration proof

verifiedAt.description: When an Owner/Admin verified the lead

handoverMode.description: When true the bot hands off to a human agent; bot stops auto-replying

createdAt.description: When the lead first messaged the bot

updatedAt.description: Last time any lead field was updated

telegramUserId.description: Telegram user ID of the lead

registeredAt.description: When the lead submitted their registration

title.description: Entry title shown in the CRM and used as RAG context heading

content.example: To register a new trading account, visit https://hfm.com and click...
content.description: Full text content (used for vector embedding)

type.description: TEXT = RAG context, LINK = external resource, TEMPLATE = bot reply template

url.description: External URL for LINK or TEMPLATE entries

status.description: Processing status — only READY entries are used by the RAG pipeline

isActive.description: Inactive entries are excluded from vector search

content.example: To register a new trading account, visit...

type.description: Entry type: TEXT (RAG context), LINK (external resource), TEMPLATE (bot button reply)

url.description: External URL for LINK/TEMPLATE entries (GDrive, YouTube, S3, etc.)

command.description: URL-safe slug, also used as Telegram /command

label.description: Button label shown in Telegram inline keyboard

description.example: Step-by-step guide to register your HFM account
description.description: Short description shown in Telegram command list

content.description: Tiptap JSON document — rendered as Telegram message blocks

isActive.description: Inactive menus are hidden from the Telegram /menu keyboard

order.description: Display order in the Telegram menu (ascending)

command.description: URL-safe slug. Used as Telegram bot command (e.g. /tutorial-register).

label.description: Button display label shown in Telegram inline keyboard.

description.example: Step-by-step guide to register your account
description.description: Short description shown in Telegram bot commands list.

order.description: Display order in the Telegram menu (ascending).

description.example: Step-by-step guide to register your account

content.description: Tiptap JSON document. Re-generates embedding on save.

date.description: The UTC date this snapshot covers (midnight)

newLeads.description: New leads that first messaged the bot on this date

registeredLeads.description: Leads that moved to REGISTERED status on this date

depositReported.description: Leads that reported a deposit on this date

conversions.description: Deposits confirmed by an Owner/Admin on this date

tokensUsed.description: Total OpenAI tokens consumed on this date

totalLeads.description: Running total of all leads at end of this date
Total number of leads in the CRM

registeredLeads.description: Leads who have submitted registration proof

depositReported.description: Leads who reported a deposit (DEPOSIT_REPORTED status)

depositConfirmed.description: Leads whose deposit was confirmed by an Owner/Admin (DEPOSIT_CONFIRMED status)

recentStats.description: Last 7 days of daily snapshot stats

newLeads.description: New leads that joined during this week

registeredLeads.description: Leads that moved to REGISTERED during this week

monthStart.description: First day of the calendar month (YYYY-MM-DD)

newLeads.description: New leads that joined during this month

registeredLeads.description: Leads that moved to REGISTERED during this month

previous.description: Value for the previous comparable period

changePercentage.description: Percentage change from previous to current period. Positive = growth.

newToRegistered.description: Percentage of NEW leads that became REGISTERED

registeredToReported.description: Percentage of REGISTERED leads that reported a deposit

reportedToConfirmed.description: Percentage of DEPOSIT_REPORTED leads that were confirmed

new.description: Leads in NEW status during the period

registered.description: Leads in REGISTERED status during the period

depositReported.description: Leads in DEPOSIT_REPORTED status during the period

depositConfirmed.description: Leads in DEPOSIT_CONFIRMED status during the period

date.description: Date or week start (YYYY-MM-DD) depending on timeframe granularity

newLeads.description: New leads for this data point

registered.description: Registered leads for this data point

confirmed.description: Deposit-confirmed leads for this data point

leadId.description: Lead UUID this conversation belongs to

notes.description: Optional notes about why this is good/bad
